summary: Joel Spolsky announces the expansion of Stack Overflow DevDays from five cities to ten due to overwhelming demand. The initial five-city event sold out quickly with over 2,388 registrations, including 800+ in London alone. New cities added include Boston, Austin, Los Angeles, Cambridge UK, and Amsterdam. The post details the schedule, pricing, and speaker lineup including Joel himself, Jeff Atwood, Scott Hanselman, and Jon Skeet, covering topics like Android, Python, iPhone development, and jQuery.
key_points: Stack Overflow DevDays sold out its initial five-city run so quickly that five additional cities were added to meet demand
Over 2,388 developers registered worldwide, demonstrating the strength of the Stack Overflow community's desire to connect in person
The conferences were priced accessibly at $99 US with discounted student tickets, positioning them as affordable developer events
The event doubled as a launch platform for FogBugz 7 and a new Fog Creek intern project, blending community building with product marketing
money_quotes: We've got 2388 people booked worldwide so far, including over 800 in London.

It's clear that with just five events we weren't going to be able to accomodate all the people who want to spend a day meeting online Stack Overflow friends in real life and learning a little something about some hot new programming topics.
This will be the launch event for FogBugz 7 and a new product now under development by the Fog Creek summer interns, who just arrived and are already coding away earnestly.
At the current rate, it's pretty clear these conferences will sell out long before the events themselves, so don't wait until the last minute to decide.
key_insight: The rapid sellout of Stack Overflow DevDays demonstrated that online developer communities create genuine demand for in-person connection, validating Stack Overflow's community-building approach.
